channel.title: karanjakhar.net
channel.description: I am an engineer working at the intersection of AI systems and real-world applications.

item.title: The Illusion of Empty Sacrifices: Stop Mimicking Idols the Wrong Way
item.description: Sacrifice has value only when it serves a real goal. Empty sacrifices imitate the look of discipline without the purpose that makes discipline meaningful.

item.title: It Takes Courage, But...
item.description: Courage rewards action regardless of direction, which is why real goodness requires more than passive harmlessness. Strength matters because choosing rightly only means something when you could do otherwise.

item.title: Ideal State Fallacy
item.description: The perfect time to begin or deliver never arrives. Waiting for ideal conditions often hides fear, while progress comes from acting before everything feels ready.

item.title: Duality of Mind
item.description: The inner voice is a dialogue, not a monologue. One side urges action while the other looks for excuses, and discipline comes from learning which voice to follow.

item.title: Finite vs Infinite Games
item.description: Life changes depending on whether you play for a fixed win or for continued growth. The infinite-game mindset turns meaning into a practice rather than an end state.
